content:
**Klaus Iohannis Protégé Wins Romania Presidential Election in Tight Runoff**

**Bucharest, April 28, 2025** — Romania’s presidential election concluded Sunday after a closely watched runoff, with National Liberal Party (PNL) candidate Alina Stoica narrowly defeating Social Democratic Party (PSD) contender Sorin Grindeanu. The final tally, announced by the Central Electoral Bureau on Monday morning, gave Stoica 51.7% of the vote to Grindeanu’s 48.3%.

Stoica, a former justice minister and vocal anti-corruption advocate, campaigned on a platform of judicial reform and continued alignment with the European Union. Her victory is widely seen as an endorsement of outgoing President Klaus Iohannis’s pro-European legacy. Addressing supporters in Bucharest’s Victory Square late Sunday, Stoica pledged to “build a fairer, more transparent Romania for all citizens and protect our democratic institutions from backsliding.”

Grindeanu conceded defeat, but warned that “the new president must work for all Romanians, not just the elites in Bucharest.” Voter turnout reached 62%, the highest in a decade, reflecting public anxiety over corruption and the cost of living crisis.

Analysts say Stoica’s win will reassure Brussels as Romania pushes to join the EU’s Schengen area fully and seeks increased investment. However, with parliament still fragmented, she will face challenges advancing her legislative agenda. “Stoica will need to build broad coalitions if she wants to deliver real change,” said political scientist Andrei Lupu.

The official inauguration ceremony is scheduled for May 20.
